Ad Text:
Do you enjoy solving complex data challenges, leading meaningful projects, and helping people turn ideas into impact? Are you comfortable connecting big-picture strategy to managing details, timelines, and teams? If so, you might be our next Engagement Manager.

At InterWorks, we help clients make sense of data, AI, and analytics. As an Engagement Manager, you'll combine your technical expertise with strong project leadership to guide clients through data transformation. You'll connect strategy to execution and ideas to outcomes, bringing clarity, collaboration, and craft to every engagement.  This role is equal parts architect, advisor, and project manager. You'll help shape solutions, oversee delivery, and ensure that every project we take on reflects the best of InterWorks.

Location Preferences: We’d love for you to join us from one of our offices in Melbourne or Sydney.  

Salary Range: $100,000 - $150,000 AUD depending on experience and qualifications.

What You’ll Do

Lead analytics and data projects from start to finish, ensuring outcomes align with client goals

Serve as the main point of contact for clients, managing expectations, communication, and long-term relationships

Translate strategy into delivery plans, scoping, scheduling, and coordinating across teams to bring solutions to life

Provide project management oversight to ensure timelines, budgets, and quality stay on track

Act as a subject matter expert within projects for your area of focus (BI, data engineering, cloud, or governance)

Conceptualize, design, and deliver scalable analytics solutions using modern data tools and platforms

Collaborate with leadership on proposals and pre-sales, shaping scopes of work and delivery approaches

Support change management and help clients adopt new technologies, processes, and mindsets

Mentor consultants and engineers, guiding both project work and professional growth

What You’ll Need

Five or more years of experience delivering analytics or data solutions in a consulting or enterprise environment

Proven ability to lead complex projects with multiple stakeholders and dependencies

Strong project management skills — PMP, Agile, or similar certification is a plus

Hands-on experience with analytics and database tools such as Tableau, Power BI, ThoughtSpot, Snowflake, BigQuery, Redshift, or Databricks

Familiarity with data orchestration and transformation tools (dbt, Matillion, Fivetran, Alteryx, or Python)

Comfort with cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, or Google Cloud)

Excellent communication and storytelling skills, from executive briefings to technical discussions

Experience with pre-sales processes and scoping project work

A track record of bringing clarity to complexity and driving projects to completion

A collaborative mindset that builds strong partnerships across teams and with clients

What We’d Like You to Have

Experience leading data modernisation or governance initiatives

A background in analytics platform design or embedded analytics

Change management or organisational transformation experience

A passion for mentoring others and helping teams grow and deliver great work together

What We Offer

Health Benefits: Our people are our most important asset. That's why we offer an exceptional health insurance subsidy through BUPA, ensuring you and your family are well taken care of.

Wellness Benefits: We want you to stay healthy! Our employees receive an annual allowance to spend on their favourite gym memberships, sports activities, or workout apps.

Work-Life Balance: We offer a life balance allowance, encouraging our employees to relax and recharge — whether with family or going solo.

Tech Choice: Windows or Mac? 13-inch or 15-inch? Lightweight or heavy-duty? You know what works best for you. Our employees have a wide choice of equipment, plus a contribution towards standing desk equipment.

Professional Growth: We invest in our people. You'll have access to ongoing training, certifications, and professional development opportunities to keep your skills sharp and your career moving forward.

Community Impact: We InterWorkers like to use our powers for good. As well as undertaking pro bono consulting work, we support our colleagues' individual charitable passions with dedicated time off for volunteering.

Superannuation: Compulsory superannuation contributions are paid on top of your base salary in accordance with Australian legislation.




Why InterWorks

InterWorks is a people-focused tech consultancy that empowers clients with customized, collaborative solutions, and we love pursuing innovation alongside people who inspire us. Our approach to work and community is unique and unconventional—just like us—and that’s the way we want it. The only thing missing is you.  At InterWorks, we value unique contributions, our people are the glue that holds our business together.  We’re always looking for the right people, and we could be your perfect fit.

Senior Data Engineer | 12-Month Contract

Location: Sydney (Hybrid/On-site)

Rate: Excellent Daily Rate

Industry: Reputed Banking & Financial Services

The Opportunity

As a Data Engineer, you will be a key contributor to the design and development of high-performance data pipelines and platform features. This role sits at the heart of our transformational data initiatives, where you will apply advanced problem-solving skills to build practical, well-structured solutions that underpin the bank's future data capabilities.


Key Responsibilities

Feed Configuration: Design and write complex configurations for diverse data feeds, ensuring seamless integration and data consistency across the platform.

Pipeline Engineering: Build and maintain scalable, reliable data pipelines using Python, SQL, and dbt.

Cloud Architecture: Develop and optimize data solutions within AWS, specifically leveraging Amazon Redshift.

Stakeholder Alignment: Work directly with business and operational stakeholders to translate complex requirements into structured technical solutions.

What You Offer (Technical Stack)

Programming & SQL: Strong, hands-on Python expertise for medium-to-complex applications, paired with advanced SQL for complex querying and database structuring.

Cloud & Warehousing: Proven experience in AWS environments, specifically working with Amazon Redshift and Linux/Unix systems.

Modern Data Tooling: Proficiency with dbt for data transformation and containerization (Docker/Kubernetes).

Orchestration: Experience with pipeline tools such as Airflow or Argo Workflows.

Big Data & Integration: Familiarity with APIs and big data technologies (Spark, Hive, or Presto).

Security & CI/CD: A solid understanding of security best practices and version control using Bitbucket.

Preferred Experience

Banking Background: Previous experience in a banking or highly regulated financial environment is highly preferred.


If this sounds aligns well with what you do - Click Apply or share your resume with riddhi.moitra@randstaddigital.com.au

At Randstad, we are passionate about providing equal employment opportunities and embracing diversity to the benefit of all. We actively encourage applications from any background.

Salary: Excellent Salary + Benefits + Flexible work option
Ad Text:
Opportunity to work with one of the largest General Insurer in Australia

Open for Sydney candidates only - Hybrid work flexibility

Lead and grow a high-performing analytics team to deliver impactful insights

About Hollard

Hollard Insurance is part of the Hollard Group of Companies and one of largest General Insurer in Australia and New Zealand. We underwrite a broad range of general insurance products, including motor, home, contents, business, bicycle and pet, in proud partnership with some of Australia's biggest consumer brands and broker businesses. As an independent and privately owned company, we believe we can go further together – for our people, customers, communities and partners – through an innovative approach to providing insurance. 

Our state-of-the-art facilities in North Sydney reflect our commitment to providing innovative workspaces that support hybrid and remote work.

Your team

You'll be joining the Chief Data Analytics Office, a specialised team of analytical experts who help drive data-driven decision making across Hollard. We're a collaborative group of technical specialists who thrive on transforming complex data into actionable business insights that deliver measurable impact. Our team is built on a foundation of analytical rigour, continuous innovation, and strategic thinking – focused on developing enterprise-wide intelligence capabilities that support business growth and operational excellence.

Your role

Reporting to the Business Insights & Reporting Lead, you will contribute to the success of the function through the following key responsibilities:e

Your duties and responsibilities

Execute enterprise business intelligence strategies in partnership with the Business Insights and Reporting leadership team

Collaborate with business stakeholders across Hollard to prioritise, scope out and deliver high-value reporting and analytical initiatives that measurably improve operational performance

Lead complex insights and advanced analytical programs that support business strategy, ameasurable business outcomes and operational efficiencies

Implement continuous improvement processes that reduce reporting cycle times, enhance reliability and user experience 

Establish performance benchmarks and implement feedback loops to ensure all reporting deliverables and benchmarks meet evolving business needs and regulatory requirements

Lead a team by setting clear performance expectations, conducting regular one-on-ones, and creating individual development plans that align with business objectives and career progression

Your knowledge and experience 

Demonstrated experience in an analytical/reporting role within the general insurance industry or financial services with demonstrated ability to deliver measurable business outcomes.

Proven ability to lead and inspire analytical teams through complex projects, fostering a culture of coaching, motivation and high performance.

Strong experience in business intelligence, project management, and reporting with ability to design scalable processes and frameworks that improve team efficiency and stakeholder satisfaction

Advanced technical proficiency in analytical tools (SAS, SQL, Python) with strong statistical capability, experience in visualisation platforms (PowerBI, Tableau) is required.

Strategic thinking capabilities with ability to balance long-term analytical vision with immediate business needs and priorities, ensuring impactful delivery across the organisation.

Working at Hollard

At Hollard, you’ll discover an environment where you can truly make an impact. We offer the best of both worlds – the opportunity, agility and creativity of a company growing with scale, alongside a focus on becoming a catalyst for enduring, positive, social change. 

We’re a tight-knit team, where authentic voices and unique perspectives matter. We work supportively, collaboratively and inclusively to help shape our future and create shared value for our communities. 

Hollard proudly promotes flexible work including remote work, non-traditional work patterns, and work-family balance. We’re also unapologetically committed to creating a workplace that values your diversity with a true sense of inclusion.  We actively seek to hire for your different lived experiences, including as it relates to disability, ethnicity, faith, Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Peoples, gender diversity, sexual orientation, age, neurodiversity. 

We’re building something special. Working closely with our industry partners to reshape insurance for the better, we’re people changing insurance. 

Please note:  Even if you don’t meet all our selection criteria, we encourage you to apply.  

A full background check will be completed on the successful applicant. 

Visit the Hollard careers page to learn about how Hollard will collect, store and handle your personal information. 

We are NOT engaging Recruitment Agencies for this role and ask agencies to respect our policy of no unsolicited contact regarding advertised vacancies.

Do you want a job that looks different every day? Have you got an unquenchable passion for data and problem solving? Are you ready to jump into a work environment where you'll collaborate with some of the sharpest minds in the industry? We've got a career you'll enjoy.

At InterWorks, we like our work with a hefty side of play. Our team of high achievers is looking for an eager learner who doesn't shy away from hard work. We need someone whose overall data and programming savvy outweighs niche experience with a particular database platform. Whether you've worked primarily in SQL Server, Postgres, or a more modern cloud-based warehouse, all we really care about is that you have a strong understanding of building data pipelines and are ready to keep learning, every single day.

Please be advised that this role is required to be located in the InterWorks Melbourne office in Melbourne, VIC. Remote work or telecommuting arrangements outside of this jurisdiction are not permissible for this position.

Valid work visa or Australian work rights are required for this position.

Salary range commensurate with experience and qualifications: $100,000-$150,000 AUD

What You'll Do

Tackle diverse projects ranging in duration from a few days to a few months, for clients ranging from local Perth businesses to global enterprises and ASX-listed companies

Work with disparate data sources (relational databases, flat files, Excel, HDFS/Big Data systems, high-performance analytical databases, etc.) to unify client data

Collaborate closely with users to understand their unique needs and support them with the best solutions

Solve data-acquisition, integration, and management problems

Create ETL processes based on client needs while managing client expectations

What You'll Need

Must-Haves

Excellent SQL fluency

Strong ETL proficiency using GUI-based tools or code-based patterns

Understanding of data-modelling principles

Passion for delivering compelling solutions that exceed client expectations

Excellent verbal and written communication skills

Strong problem-solving skills

Ability to translate business problems into practical data solutions and work effectively with non-technical stakeholders

A genuine thirst to learn

Adaptability and flexibility in changing situations

What We'd Like You to Have

Experience with modern data-engineering practices and frameworks

Experience with integration from semi-structured file and API sources

Familiarity with tools such as Informatica (IDMC), Matillion, Fivetran, or dbt

Experience with cloud platforms such as Snowflake, Databricks, Amazon Redshift, Google BigQuery, or Azure Synapse

What We Offer:

Health Benefits: Our people are our most important asset. That's why we offer an exceptional health insurance subsidy through BUPA, ensuring you and your family are well taken care of.

Wellness Benefits: We want you to stay healthy! Our employees receive an annual allowance to spend on their favourite gym memberships, sports activities, or workout apps.

Work-Life Balance: We offer a life balance allowance, encouraging our employees to relax and recharge — whether with family or going solo.

Tech Choice: Windows or Mac? 13-inch or 15-inch? Lightweight or heavy-duty? You know what works best for you. Our employees have a wide choice of equipment, plus a contribution towards standing desk equipment.

Professional Growth: We invest in our people. You'll have access to ongoing training, certifications, and professional development opportunities to keep your skills sharp and your career moving forward.

Community Impact: We InterWorkers like to use our powers for good. As well as undertaking pro bono consulting work, we support our colleagues' individual charitable passions with dedicated time off for volunteering.

Superannuation: Compulsory superannuation contributions are paid on top of your base salary in accordance with Australian legislation.

Why InterWorks

InterWorks is a people-focused tech consultancy that empowers clients with customized, collaborative solutions, and we love pursuing innovation alongside people who inspire us. Our approach to work and community is unique and unconventional—just like us—and that's the way we want it. The only thing missing is you. At InterWorks, we value unique contributions, our people are the glue that holds our business together. We're always looking for the right people, and we could be your perfect fit.

Technical Business Analyst

Sydney | Hybrid | Immediate Start

A leading Australian digital media organisation is looking for an experienced Technical Business Analyst for an initial 6-month contract. This is an exciting opportunity to work within a high-performing product and technology environment supporting commercial platforms used at scale.

Reporting to the Head of Product - Commercial Platforms, this role sits within the technical delivery team while working closely with Sales and commercial stakeholders. You will play a key role translating business needs into technical solutions, improving processes, and ensuring systems effectively support a fast-moving commercial function.
This role suits a proactive Business Analyst who thrives in dynamic environments, enjoys stakeholder engagement, and is comfortable balancing multiple priorities.

Key Responsibilities
Partner with Product, Technology, and Sales teams to gather, analyse, and document business and technical requirements
Translate commercial and operational needs into clear functional and technical specifications
Facilitate workshops and stakeholder discussions across technical and non-technical teams
Perform SQL troubleshooting and data analysis to support issue investigation and solution validation
Develop detailed process maps and identify opportunities for optimisation and efficiency
Support delivery teams throughout the project lifecycle including discovery, delivery, testing, and rollout
Assist with backlog refinement, prioritisation, and solution clarification
Drive clear communication between business stakeholders and engineering teams
Skills & Experience Required
Proven experience as a Technical Business Analyst within digital, media, technology, or commercial platform environments
Strong SQL skills with experience troubleshooting data or system issues
Advanced process mapping and workflow documentation capabilities
Excellent stakeholder engagement and communication skills, particularly with Sales or commercial teams
Ability to operate effectively in a fast-paced, evolving environment
Strong multitasking and prioritisation skills across competing initiatives
Experience working alongside Product Managers and Engineering teams in Agile delivery environments
Confident facilitating workshops and managing stakeholder expectations
Highly Regarded
Experience supporting advertising, sales, or commercial platforms
Exposure to media, publishing, or digital content environments
Familiarity with Agile methodologies (Scrum/Kanban) and tools such as Jira or Confluence
Data-driven mindset with strong analytical problem-solving skills
What’s on Offer
6-month contract with potential extension
Hybrid working model based in Sydney
Opportunity to work on high-visibility platforms used by large audiences
Collaborative, fast-moving product and technology environment
Strong leadership exposure working closely with senior product stakeholders

If this sounds like you, apply today!

Shape how we build, connect and deliver
At Icon, we’re building more than projects, we’re building a smarter, more connected construction business.

With a strong pipeline across health, education, defence and large scale residential, we’re investing in our digital capability to transform how we plan, deliver and operate projects. This includes modernising our applications, establishing a scalable data platform, and embedding AI and automation into everyday workflows.

We’re looking for an Applications & Digital Manager to assist in leading this journey.
  
The Opportunity
This is a high impact role sitting at the intersection of strategy and delivery.
You will own the applications and digital roadmap, while also stepping in to deliver early outcomes and working solutions. This role is suited to someone who can define the plan — and then roll up their sleeves to make it real.
You’ll work closely with senior leaders, project teams, and functional stakeholders to modernise systems, improve decision making, and embed smarter, more efficient ways of working across the business.
  
What You’ll Be Responsible For:
Applications & Platform Planning

Lead and deliver the applications and digital roadmap aligned to business priorities
Rationalise and optimise our application landscape across corporate, project, and site environments
Establish scalable architecture, integration, and platform standards
Data Platform & Reporting

Lead the establishment of an enterprise data platform (e.g. Snowflake or equivalent)
Integrate data across project, operational, commercial, and finance systems
Enable consistent, self service reporting and dashboards (Power BI)
Improve visibility of project performance, risk, and commercial outcomes
AI & Automation

Identify and deliver practical AI and automation use cases that improve productivity and project outcomes
Drive adoption of Microsoft Copilot and other AI tools across the business
Embed automation into high volume, time intensive workflows with experience in automation tools (e.g. Power Automate, Workato or others).
Delivery & Capability Build

Deliver initial solutions hands on where needed to establish direction and momentum
Build and lead a small, high performing digital team over time
Partner with vendors and internal teams to scale delivery capability
  Governance & Outcomes

Drive standardisation, reduce duplication, and improve system efficiency
Ensure solutions are secure, scalable, and aligned to operational realities
Provide clear reporting on progress, risks, and business outcomes
About You
You’re a pragmatic digital leader who combines structured thinking with hands on delivery.

Proven experience leading applications, data, or digital transformation initiatives
Strong ability to translate business needs into clear, actionable roadmaps
Hands on advanced experience delivering dashboards, integrations, automation, data platform solutions including experience in Snowflake, Data bricks and Power BI.
Experience working across modern Microsoft ecosystems, data platforms, and integration tools, including ETL and API integration (TSQL, Python and JSON).
Comfortable operating in a lean, delivery focused environment
Strong stakeholder engagement skills across technical and operational teams
  Benefits and perks:

Access to our EAP (Employee Assistance Program)
Market competitive Salary and excellent working environment
Excellent Leave Polices that include purchased Annual Leave, Work Life Balance Days, Community Days and generous Parental Leave scheme
Collaborative and values-based workplace culture
Sporting and Social events
Professional Memberships
Discounts on Travel, Retail and Health
How to apply
Applications should be made by submitting a CV and cover letter online outlining your interest, motivation and what you would bring to this role by way of background.

Recruiters, please note we are not engaging agency support on this role unless directly contacted by our internal recruitment team. If you have not been approached, we are not seeking external support at this time. Unsolicited CVs will not be accepted and will be disregarded. Candidates may be contacted directly by our internal team.

Elastic, the Search AI Company, enables everyone to find the answers they need in real time, using all their data, at scale — unleashing the potential of businesses and people. The Elastic Search AI Platform, used by more than 50% of the Fortune 500, brings together the precision of search and the intelligence of AI to enable everyone to accelerate the results that matter. By taking advantage of all structured and unstructured data — securing and protecting private information more effectively — Elastic’s complete, cloud-based solutions for search, security, and observability help organizations deliver on the promise of AI.

What is The Role:


Elastic, the Search Analytics company, is looking for a Principal Solution Architect (pre-sales). Are you looking to make a real impact and play a meaningful role in the growth of our company? As a Solutions Architect (SA) at Elastic, you will serve as a technical authority and trusted advisor to our sales team, customers, partners and community. You will understand and solve our customers’ business issues with the Elastic Stack, and engage the regional Elastic community through events and programs. A successful SA at Elastic will be focused on excellence, taking the initiative to improve both themselves and the team through continuous learning and questioning the status quo.

What You Will Be Doing:

Serving as the technical point of contact for some of our clients
Developing a deep understanding of customers’ goals and objectives, and articulating how our offerings address their needs.
Creating and owning value-based relationships at all levels in customer organisations.
Actively participating in all phases of planning and execution for your territory, from initial discovery to the technical win.
Developing and maintaining a deep understanding of the Elastic products and solutions to demonstrate the value of our offerings in sales meetings and at events such as meetups and conferences.
Advising the sales team on effective ways of positioning Elastic products, solutions and services.
Onboarding, educating and enabling our partners, and supporting them in sales cycles.
Creating collateral, contributing to programs and collaborating with other Elasticians to meet individual client needs.
Being the voice of the customer and community to communicate needs, gaps, and enhancements to our engineering and leadership teams.
Deepening both your sales and technical skills through self-driven education while taking advantage of all the professional development opportunities provided by Elastic.
What You Bring:

A track record of success in a technical presales role – enough experience in selling, demonstrating and implementing technology to earn your customer’s trust.
Experience in at least one of those technologies/solutions is a must: AI enterprise search, observability or cybersecurity
Experience in working with large or mid-size organisations
A demonstrable ability to articulate and sell the benefits of modern platforms, software and technologies.
A real passion for being curious and a continuous learner. You are someone who invests in yourself as much as you invest in your professional relationships.
A history of successful customer relations where you developed an understanding of what made a difference, and devised architectures that helped meet a goal, tackle a problem, or outpace competitors.
An ability to influence. Have you more than once convinced a team you worked for of an idea, technology, or architectural pattern?
The ability to inspire groups, both large and small.
A willingness to travel 30% within a region, as well as occasionally internationally.
Fluent in English (business or native level)
Additional Information - We Take Care of Our People:


As a distributed company, diversity drives our identity. Whether you’re looking to launch a new career or grow an existing one, Elastic is the type of company where you can balance great work with great life. Your age is only a number. It doesn’t matter if you’re just out of college or your children are; we need you for what you can do.

We strive to have parity of benefits across regions, and while regulations differ from place to place, we believe taking care of our people is the right thing to do.

Competitive pay based on the work you do here and not your previous salary
Health coverage for you and your family in many locations
Ability to craft your calendar with flexible locations and schedules for many roles
Generous number of vacation days each year
Increase your impact - We match up to $2000 (or local currency equivalent) for financial donations and service
Up to 40 hours each year to use toward volunteer projects you love
Embracing parenthood with a minimum of 16 weeks of parental leave

Different people approach problems differently. We need that. Elastic is an equal opportunity employer and is committed to creating an inclusive culture that celebrates different perspectives, experiences, and backgrounds. Qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, ethnicity, color, religion, sex, pregnancy, sexual orientation, gender perception or identity, national origin, age, marital status, protected veteran status, disability status, or any other basis protected by federal, state or local law, ordinance or regulation.

We welcome individuals with disabilities and strive to create an accessible and inclusive experience for all individuals. To request an accommodation during the application or the recruiting process, please email candidate_accessibility@elastic.co. We will reply to your request within 24 business hours of submission.

Applicants have rights under Federal Employment Laws and can view the following posters linked below:

Family and Medical Leave Act (FMLA) Poster

Employee Polygraph Protection Act (EPPA) Poster

Elasticsearch develops and distributes technology and information that is subject to U.S. and other countries’ export controls and licensing requirements for individuals who are located in or are nationals of the following sanctioned countries and regions: Belarus, Cuba, Iran, North Korea, Syria, or Russia, including the Ukrainian territories annexed by Russia (The Crimea region of Ukraine, The Donetsk People's Republic (DNR), The Luhansk People's Republic (LNR), Kherson or Zaporizhzhia). If you are located in or are a national of one of the listed countries or regions, an export license may be required as a condition of your employment in this role. Please note that national origin and/or nationality do not affect eligibility for employment with Elastic.

Job Title: MD/Director – M&A – Independent Corporate Advisory Firm
Category: Corporate Finance & Investment Banking (Banking & Financial Services)
Ad Text:
Lead a high-performing advisory team with some of the strongest ASX board level connections in Aus
Own client management and lead execution with support from well-known senior rainmakers
Play a pivotal role in platform growth and direction

‍

Our client is an independent advisory platform with over 25 years of experience within the Aus market. They are seeking a commercially astute Corporate Advisory Director to help drive continued growth and oversee deal execution. The platform benefits from strong networks and a reputation for high‑quality, independent advice, offering unique exposure across M&A, capital management, corporate strategy and special situations.

‍

Ad Text:
The role: 

You will be responsible for engaging and communicating effectively with stakeholders at every level (Director, Data Manager, Team Lead, Clinicians, and end users) when required to make sure a complete implicit understanding of the business is known. 

You will need to proactively encourage key difficult discussions with the right stakeholders in order to fully comprehend the important aspects of the client's needs and making sure all facets are examined from all angles. This will be important for interpreting and translating technical possibilities into clinical suitability. 

Reporting to the Project Director, you will be responsible for capturing and analysing “As-Is” and “To-Be” business processes models and capturing the business requirements to ensure traceability of requirements throughout the project lifecycle. You will ensure requirements are captured and addressed in the transition of the service from Project to Business As usual whilst providing Business Analysis functions at the Program level, as and when required.

Additionally, you will contribute to an effective client focused team and work collaboratively to initiate, influence and maintain relationships with and between internal and external stakeholders.

Are you right for this role?

To be successful in this role, you must have demonstrated experience performing business analysis activities within IM/ICT projects preferably in the health care industry. You will also have experience with managing stakeholder relationships throughout the project delivery lifecycle, in addition to experience in capturing and analysing “As-Is' and ‘To-Be' business process models across large organisations, eliciting and documenting business and software requirement specifications.

You will have the proven ability to work within a team environment, developing effective working relationships with team members and stakeholders including mentoring other business analysts within the organisation. Proven effective consultation, facilitation, written and oral communication skills for use in a project environment is essential. 

You will also be a self-motivated problem solver, who has proven experience in driving pragmatic decision making and delivering a wide range of documentation to meet the needs of the target audience.

A high level of knowledge of and the ability to provide advice on the application of appropriate business analysis techniques, methodologies and tools, preferably aligned with Business Analysis Body of Knowledge Version 3.0 (BABOK).

About Better Hospital Flow (BHF) Project:

The Queensland Government is investing in improved hospital services through real-time data and targeted technology upgrades under the Health Plan Commitment. A key priority action is Better Hospital Flows (GEC124), which focuses on modernising digital systems to improve hospital workflows, patient flow and visibility of bed availability across the state. Delivery of GEC124 is being progressed through two related initiatives:

Project 3a, which focuses on enhancements to the Statewide Management Information System (SWMIS) to improve the timeliness, quality and consistency of core operational data; and
Project 3b, which focuses on the assessment, planning and implementation of a statewide Patient Flow Insights capability that translates operational data into actionable insights.

Government Election Commitment (GEC) 124 – Better Hospital Flows – Project 3b comprises two phases: Phase 1 - Assessment and Phase 2 - Implementation.
This role relates to Project 3b: Assessment and Implementation and hereafter refers to it as the “project”.

Competitive salary + generous superannuation and leave loading
